title: Trading on Ninja Trader with an agent.
author: BulkySeries4173
selftext: Hey hey!! I’m fairly new with AI development and have some experience with trading futures. Help me out here. Hypothetically does the technology exist to develop an agent that can scalp 10 pips on futures such as oil, gold, silver, and/or NASDAQ? If so what are some strategies that could correlate with accomplishing this goal? I’m thinking Claude code and Ninja Trader for technology. Let me know your thoughts.

comments.author: Otherwise_Wave9374
comments.body:
Tech exists, but the hard part is not getting an "agent" to place orders, it is making it robust (latency, slippage, regime shifts, risk limits) and proving it with honest backtests and forward tests.

If you go agentic, I would treat it like: (1) deterministic strategy logic, (2) agent only for research/monitoring/explanation, and (3) hard risk controls outside the agent (max loss/day, max position, kill switch). Let the agent suggest, but keep execution constrained.

If you want ideas on how people structure tool using agents with guardrails, there are some practical notes here: https://www.agentixlabs.com/blog/

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body: yeah the tech def exists, people have been running algo scalpers on NT for years. the hard part isn’t coding it in Claude, it’s latency, slippage, and fees — 10 pips on something like NQ can disappear fast if your fills aren’t clean. ngl you’ll probably spend more time tuning risk management and execution than the actual “strategy” logic.

comments.author: Patient-Bumblebee
comments.body:
Yes its easy bro, just write a prompt for your strategy and plug it into a free prompt trading platform like [Everstrike](https://everstrike.io).  

Crypto only so wouldnt be able to trade Nasdaq but you could trade gold using the PAXG symbol.

comments.author: bjxxjj
comments.body: yeah the tech def exists, people have been running automated scalpers on NT for years. the hard part isn’t coding it with CC, it’s latency, slippage, and fees eating those 10 pips alive, especially on stuff like NQ. most of the “strategies” end up being some combo of order flow, VWAP reversion, or momentum bursts, but edge is thin tbh.

comments.author: Forsaken_Leader_8
comments.body: There's a lot of free ai trading tools or at least tools that actually pay for itself, like analysis your charts or Track congress news, give you signals daily. Stuff like that. Personally I have been using signal whisper for a while now
